status: documentation only; no mapper code changes
purpose: Determine which remaining blocked PRISM nodes can be responsibly mapped from CCES alone, which require ANES, and which are inherent fallbacks.
coverage_baseline: results/electorate/synthetic-electorate/survey-to-prism-mapper-coverage-audit.{md,json} (Phase 2.7.7) — confirms all 10 audited nodes at 0% real-signal coverage across all 5 cycles.
explicitly_not_in_this_audit: No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data / candidate / era-context edits.
forbidden_universal_inputs: pid7 for any core PRISM position node — only allowed as flagged signal for moralBoundaries.political_camp
Partisan-camp-loaded items (Kavanaugh confirmation, Trump impeachment, ACB confirmation) for PRO/ONT_S — would inject political_camp circularity

nodes.verdict: Light cross-load already in CU composite
nodes.v02_path: Composite-design: lift existing CU-item cross-loads to a primary MOR composite. Data already in mapper; just routing work.

nodes.definition: Human malleability: 1=fixed by nature, 5=shaped by environment

nodes.anes_path: Feldman-Stenner authoritarian-parenting battery (V162267-V162272 in 2016; equivalent in 2020) — independence vs respect for elders, curiosity vs good manners, etc.
nodes.v01_v02_status: Continue uniform fallback prior; cleanest 'ANES-only' node in the audit

implementation_impact_v01.blocker_count_after_v01: 10 → 4 (still blocked: PRO, COM, ONT_H, EPS — all ANES-required)

terminology: moralBoundaries.ethnic_racial, moralBoundaries.gender, moralBoundaries.political_camp are the canonical compound moral-circle boundaries per ADR-006. Engagement is referenced only as a downstream concern — separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon — not surfaced by any item audited here. Legacy code identifiers (pid7, ideo5, CC*_*, VCF0604 etc.) appear only as CCES / ANES variable names.

revision_history.description: Initial audit of 10 hard-node CCES sources. 3 mappable in v0.1, 3 deferred to v0.2 with composite-design work, 4 require ANES, 1 inherent fallback (AES).
